title: Acceptance suite never validates the app under scoring (test_coverage=0.14, app.py ~0% covered)
evidence: test_app.py:13 and conftest.py:17 issue HTTP requests to a live server, but no fixture/harness starts one; scores.json test_coverage=0.14 and .coverage shows app.py essentially unexercised — all 25 tests error on connection when run headless via `pytest --cov`.
suggestion: Start the Flask app in-process for tests (Flask test_client or a session-scoped fixture that launches/tears down the server) so the acceptance suite is self-hosting and actually exercises app.py.

title: Port mismatch: app.py binds 5000 but tests/conftest/README target 5001
evidence: app.py:217 `app.run(..., port=5000, ...)` vs test_app.py:13 and conftest.py:8 `BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:5001` and README.md:73 'server starts at 5001'. Following the README, `python app.py` binds 5000 and every test fails to connect.
suggestion: Make the port consistent — bind 5000→5001 in app.py (or read from env) so the documented run instructions actually make the tests pass.

title: R12: >=3 tests exist but do not effectively run/pass (coverage 0.14)
evidence: 25 tests defined in test_app.py, but they require an externally-started server on the wrong port, so none pass under scoring; test_coverage=0.14 barely clears >0 and no behavior is actually verified.
suggestion: Make at least the core CRUD/health tests self-hosting so they pass without manual setup, satisfying 'tests exist and run'.

title: Flask app runs with debug=True
evidence: app.py:217 `app.run(host='0.0.0.0', port=5000, debug=True)` — the Werkzeug interactive debugger allows arbitrary code execution and binds on all interfaces.
suggestion: Disable debug (or gate behind an env flag) and avoid binding 0.0.0.0 for a local service.

title: README run instructions are inaccurate (wrong port)
evidence: README.md:73 states the server starts at http://127.0.0.1:5001, but app.py:217 binds 5000; the documented 'python app.py' + pytest workflow cannot succeed as written.
suggestion: Fix the README port or the app port so the run/test instructions are self-consistent.

title: R1-R11 implemented: full CRUD + filter + validation + health + SQLite
evidence: app.py implements POST/GET-list/GET-id/PUT/DELETE (app.py:100-210), ?author= filter (app.py:139-145), title/author validation (app.py:67-89), /health (app.py:93-96), SQLite persistence (app.py:40-53). README present.
suggestion: No action — implementation is complete and idiomatic; the gap is test executability, not functionality.
